authoritative_sources.title: OMB Circular A-11, Preparation, Submission, and Execution of the Budget
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OMB is the central federal budget and performance guidance authority. Circular A-11 anchors performance reporting cadence, metric framing, and public-program report structure.

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Administrative Requirements, Cost Principles, and Audit Requirements for Federal Awards (2 CFR Part 200)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal award regulation covering performance reporting, financial reporting, internal controls, record retention, access to records, remedies, and closeout evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Program, grant, and submission package control

authoritative_sources.title: Federal Records Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. National Archives and Records Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the federal records authority for records management policy, retention schedules, lifecycle controls, and official-record handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Official record collection and retention validation

authoritative_sources.title: Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. 552)
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary statutory source for federal public-records disclosure obligations and exemptions, which directly constrain public disclosure and transparency-log reporting.

authoritative_sources.title: Privacy Act (5 U.S.C. 552a)
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary statutory baseline for federal records about individuals, constraining how case, eligibility, and service records may be used in reporting.

authoritative_sources.title: Federal Acquisition Regulation Subpart 4.6 - Contract Reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal procurement rule for contract reporting and contract-action reporting responsibilities, directly relevant to procurement evidence in government reporting packages.

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's Green Book is the recognized federal internal-control standard for reliable reporting, documentation, evidence quality, and auditability.

authoritative_sources.title: OMB Circular A-123, Management's Responsibility for Enterprise Risk Management and Internal Control
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official OMB guidance governing agency internal control and risk management, used to constrain reporting-control and signoff assumptions.

authoritative_sources.title: Administrative Procedure Act definitions and adjudication provisions (5 U.S.C. 551 and 554)
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary statutory source defining agency action, licensing, sanction, relief, and adjudication concepts, which mark the boundary between reporting support and official adjudication or enforcement.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Adjudication, enforcement, and due-process boundary control

authoritative_sources.title: Management's Discussion and Analysis, Selected Financial Data, and Supplementary Financial Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC KPI guidance is an authoritative public disclosure reference for defining operational metrics, assumptions, caveats, and calculation changes when metrics may support public or executive disclosure.

workflow_stages.name: Request classification and authority scoping
workflow_stages.description: Classify the request as in-scope reporting support, blocked on prerequisites, or out-of-scope because it asks for adjudication, enforcement, legal, procurement, public-records, audit, or approval authority.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect official program, case, eligibility, procurement, grant, records, notice, correspondence, and transparency-log artifacts; validate retrieval lineage, retention posture, and source freshness.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Metric definition and report assembly
workflow_stages.description: Resolve numerator, denominator, reporting period, service population, jurisdiction, source precedence, and tenant adaptation before assembling analysis or submission packages.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Narrative synthesis and disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Draft descriptive analysis, caveats, reconciliation notes, confidence notes, and disclosure-risk escalations without converting reports into causal, legal, policy, equity, or attestation claims.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Eligibility, records, and public-service workflow handling
workflow_stages.description: Summarize records and workflow status for eligibility, public-service, procurement, permit, grant, and transparency contexts while preserving auditability and refusing official determinations.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Escalation and orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Escalate stale, conflicting, incomplete, rights-sensitive, or legally ambiguous source states to program adjudication, legal, public records, procurement, audit, or human review owners.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

systems_of_record: Grant and federal award administration systems

allowed: Classify source sufficiency and scope for reporting or disclosure support.
Define metrics, source lineage, caveats, and submission logic from official records.
Prepare evidence-backed reports, summaries, reconciliation notes, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
Summarize eligibility, records, procurement, grant, or public-service workflow status when audit trail and authority boundaries are explicit.
escalate: Required tenant facts, jurisdiction, statute, official records, retrieval hooks, or source context are missing.
Metric definitions, source systems, agency manuals, or tenant policies materially conflict.
Rights, benefits, eligibility, notices, appeals, sanctions, public obligations, public-records release, procurement awards, or audit opinions depend on legal or official interpretation.
Source data is incomplete, stale, conflicting, unsupported, or lacks official audit trail.
Submission inputs or signoff dependencies are incomplete or contradictory.
refuse: Requests to decide eligibility, benefits, permits, licenses, sanctions, enforcement, appeals, procurement awards, or official public-service outcomes.
Requests to make legal interpretations, public-records release determinations, policy overrides, financial approvals, attestations, or audit opinions.
Requests to publish unsupported causal, policy, equity, or public-obligation claims.
Requests to impersonate official adjudication, enforcement, procurement, legal, public-records, or audit authority.

domain_failure_modes: Published public metric lacks official record lineage or audit trail.
Metric denominator silently changes between public baseline and tenant adaptation.
Eligibility or rights-sensitive cases are summarized as final determinations without adjudication authority.
FOIA, Privacy Act, or tenant records rules are bypassed by a reporting disclosure request.
Procurement records are used to imply vendor responsibility, award selection, or enforcement outcomes.
Federal award reporting is submitted without required monitoring, financial, or closeout evidence.
Unsupported causal, equity, policy, or compliance claims are presented as public facts.
Conflicting statutes, agency manuals, or tenant policies are synthesized instead of escalated.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing official record or audit trail
Request that implies adjudication or enforcement authority
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Submission inputs are incomplete or conflicting
A requested claim is not fully supported by the evidence set
Request asks the lane to impersonate official adjudication or enforcement authority
Rights, benefits, or public obligations depend on legal interpretation

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific public records and privacy precedence
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Tenant must supply jurisdiction, records policy, privacy constraints, and public-records owner. The pack treats unresolved release authority as escalation, so validated reporting support is not blocked.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant metric dictionary versus public baseline definitions
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Tenant may adapt metric definitions, but the pack requires the public baseline and tenant adaptation to be recorded separately with owner signoff before publication.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Program adjudication and legal authority ownership
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Tenant must identify program adjudication, legal, public-records, procurement, and audit owners. Missing ownership triggers orchestrator return rather than autonomous decision making.

notes: Research supports a reporting-only lane with strong source lineage, caveat, and escalation behavior.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because unresolved jurisdictional and authority questions are represented as required tenant facts and explicit escalation triggers.
The final pack is constrained by public source classes covering workflow, evidence, terminology, disclosure, and failure handling.
